title: compare_sign_neighbors_across_periods data payload
description: v0.26.0 sign2vec diachronic query tool. For a single query sign, returns the top-K nearest neighbors from the Neo-Assyrian and Neo-Babylonian period sub-corpus embeddings (same WINDOW=5 / MIN_OCC=20 hyperparameters, separately trained sub-corpora). Surfaces three drift signals: signs shared by both top-K rankings (the stable distributional core), signs unique to NA's top-K, and signs unique to NB's top-K. Non-zero na_only/nb_only counts flag the query sign as a candidate diachronic substitution — its distributional behavior changed between the Neo-Assyrian and Neo-Babylonian sub-corpora. Companion to v0.23's find_similar_signs (single corpus-wide config) and v0.25's compare_sign_embedding_configs (hyperparameter axis); this tool is the diachronic axis. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

query_sign.description: The sign code submitted by the caller, after whitespace trim.

in_na.description: True iff the query sign passes the Neo-Assyrian MIN_OCC gate and has an embedding vector there.

in_nb.description: True iff the query sign passes the Neo-Babylonian MIN_OCC gate and has an embedding vector there.

neighbors_na.description: Top-K nearest neighbors in the Neo-Assyrian embedding space (empty when in_na=false).

sign.description: Neighbor sign code (e.g. an ABZ identifier).

cosine.description: Cosine similarity between query and neighbor in the NA embedding. Vectors are L2-normalized at build time so cosine == dot product.

occurrences.description: Occurrence count of this neighbor in the Neo-Assyrian filtered sub-corpus.

neighbors_nb.description: Top-K nearest neighbors in the Neo-Babylonian embedding space (empty when in_nb=false).

sign.description: Neighbor sign code (e.g. an ABZ identifier).

cosine.description: Cosine similarity between query and neighbor in the NB embedding.

occurrences.description: Occurrence count of this neighbor in the Neo-Babylonian filtered sub-corpus.

common_neighbors.description: Sign codes appearing in BOTH NA's top-K and NB's top-K. The stable distributional core for this query sign — neighbors that survive the diachronic crossing.

na_only_neighbors.description: Sign codes in NA's top-K but not in NB's top-K. Strong candidates for Neo-Assyrian-specific co-occurrence partners that fell out of distributional reach by the Neo-Babylonian period.

nb_only_neighbors.description: Sign codes in NB's top-K but not in NA's top-K. Strong candidates for Neo-Babylonian-specific co-occurrence partners — distributional successors picked up between periods.

na_only_count.description: Count of NA-only neighbors. Higher values signal stronger diachronic drift for this query sign.

nb_only_count.description: Count of NB-only neighbors. Higher values signal stronger diachronic drift for this query sign.

na_signs_indexed.description: Number of signs above MIN_OCC in the Neo-Assyrian sub-corpus embedding (0 if the NA cache failed to load).

nb_signs_indexed.description: Number of signs above MIN_OCC in the Neo-Babylonian sub-corpus embedding (0 if the NB cache failed to load).

na_tablets_in_period.description: Number of tablets whose script.period == 'Neo-Assyrian' that contributed to the NA sub-corpus.

nb_tablets_in_period.description: Number of tablets whose script.period == 'Neo-Babylonian' that contributed to the NB sub-corpus.
